Notice Of Motion To Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 17.1and Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SHIRIN ETESSAM AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion To Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 17.1and Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (by Zoom):
Glenn Coffman, Esq., of Johnson & Johnson LLP, (310) 975-1080, for Plaintiff.
Alan Bayer, Esq., of Hakimi Law, PC, (415) 255-4503, for Defendant Shirin Etessam.
Paul Nathan, Esq., Law Offices of, (415) 341-1144, for Defendant Nancy Gaglio.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Shirin Etassem's Motion To Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 17.1 and Request For Sanctions is GRANTED.

***SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order On Defendant Nanci Gaglio'S Special Interrogatories Directed To Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Glenn Koffman, Esq. 310-975-1081 Johnson & Johnson for Plaintiff.
Paul Nathan, Esq. 415-341-1144 Law Offices for Defendant. (In-person)
Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr Coffman presents arguments; the TR hits the nail on the head, main points are in the papers, law is clear on 35 special interrogatories. Mr. Nathan contests and presents arguments; liberally construing discovery, cites Supreme court opinion, relevance, plethora of allegations, failure to meet and confer, failure to quantify burden, law of this state. Mr. Coffman presents brief rebuttal. After full arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff's motion for protective order.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***
